{"query":"Humane AI Pin HP acquisition date 2025 amount","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1347,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2025/02/18/humanes-ai-pin-is-dead-as-hp-buys-startups-assets-for-116m/","title":"Humane's AI Pin is dead, as HP buys startup's assets for $116M | TechCrunch","content":"Humane’s AI Pin is dead, as HP buys startup’s assets for $116M
Humane announced on Tuesday that most of its assets have been acquired by HP for $116 million.
The hardware startup is immediately discontinuing sales of its $499 AI Pins.
Humane alerted customers who have already purchased the Pin that their devices will stop functioning before the end of the month — at 12 p.m.
PST on February 28, 2025, according to a blog post.
After that date, the company says its AI Pins will no longer connect to Humane’s servers.
Humane made a splash in April 2024 by launching its AI Pin, which it positioned as a smartphone replacement.
The Bay Area startup, founded by ex-Apple employees Bethany Bongiorno and Imran Chaudhri, raised more than $230 million to create the device.
In October, the company, which long charged customers $699 for its AI Pin, dropped the price by $200.
Previously with Gizmodo, Bloomberg, and MSNBC, Zeff has covered the rise of AI and the Silicon Valley Bank crisis."},{"url":"https://complexdiscovery.com/the-end-of-humane-ai-pin-hps-strategic-shift-toward-ai-integration/","title":"The End of Humane AI Pin: HP’s Strategic Shift Toward AI Integration","content":"As HP reshapes Humane’s assets into a more cohesive AI strategy, this development underscores the importance of aligning cutting-edge technology with practical, user-focused applications.
A short percentage-based assessment of the qualitative benefit expressed as a percentage of positive reception of the recent article from ComplexDiscovery OÜ titled, "The End of Humane AI Pin: HP’s Strategic Shift Toward AI Integration."
The Humane AI Pin, a wearable device created by ex-Apple designers Imran Chaudhri and Bethany Bongiorno, will permanently shut down its functionality by February 28, 2025, at 12 PM PT.
The shutdown follows HP’s acquisition of Humane for $116 million—a sharp drop from previous valuations that was rumored to reach as high as $1 billion.
Learn more at ComplexDiscovery.com."},{"url":"https://www.howtogeek.com/humane-ai-pin-shutdown/","title":"Humane AI Pin Is Shutting Down After HP Acquisition","content":"Aggy has published two games under Tales and is always eager to learn and do more.
Humane, the company that developed a widely-covered AI pin, is shutting down and selling its assets to HP for $116 million.
This sale includes Humane’s CosmOS operating system, more than 300 patents and applications, and its technical staff.
As a result of the sale, the AI Pin, a product that was not well-received when it launched in April 2024, will no longer continue.
The AI Pin, which was meant to replace features from a smartphone, will stop working on February 28, 2025, at 3 PM ET.
You will get a partial refund if you have a subscription that goes beyond the shutdown date."}]}

Humane was founded by Imran Chaudhri and Bethany Bongiorno in 2018; the couple previously worked at Apple Inc. The startup emerged from stealth mode in 2021. Some 200 people were hired to work for the company, with 40% of them having spent time working at Apple Inc. The company stated that it has raised $100 million in both of its Series B and C rounds. In February 2025, it was announced that HP would acquire most of Humane Inc. for $116 million.
